nodes.label: Custom In-Repo Hugo Rebuild
nodes.rationale: A custom in-repo Hugo site preserves existing Markdown posts, post URLs, image paths, all images, and the current logo without adopting a third-party theme.

nodes.label: AI Review-Gated Publishing
nodes.rationale: AI-generated posts, images, and metadata must enter through pull requests for human review and must never publish directly to the main branch.

nodes.label: Accessible Motion Modernization
nodes.rationale: The visual refresh uses progressive parallax and scroll reveal while preserving the logo and respecting prefers-reduced-motion.

nodes.label: Hugo Migration Validation Gate
nodes.rationale: Production cutover remains gated on migration checks, Hugo builds, and staging or preview smoke tests even after all 36 Jekyll posts are represented under content/posts.

nodes.label: Default-On Vanilla Figure Lightbox
nodes.rationale: A lightweight vanilla-JavaScript lightbox avoids the unused jQuery dependency required by Lity, defaults on with a per-post opt-out, and includes keyboard and focus accessibility.

nodes.label: AI Article Pipeline MVP
nodes.rationale: The initial pipeline runs manually in GitHub Actions, generates reviewable drafts, and defers scheduling and hosted-agent promotion until the supervised phase.

nodes.label: Direct MVP Execution Topology
nodes.rationale: Direct model calls keep the MVP simple while prompts stabilize; promotion to Foundry hosted agents is deferred.

nodes.label: Least-Privilege GitHub App
nodes.rationale: The automation identity cannot self-approve pull requests, and branch protection requires Rodrigo's human approval.

nodes.label: Two-Lock Publishing Model
nodes.rationale: Rendering requires an approved pull-request merge plus draft:false and ai.reviewed_by; merging alone does not publish.

nodes.label: Per-Topic Lifecycle Ledger
nodes.rationale: One YAML file per topic records lifecycle state, while exact keys and semantic embeddings enforce a 0.82 novelty threshold against a rebuildable cache and published content index.

nodes.label: Agentic Tavily Discovery Pipeline
nodes.rationale: A ReAct tool-calling loop lets the model propose searches while the orchestrator executes Tavily, keeping its credential only in GitHub Secrets and requiring no new Azure infrastructure or Python dependencies.

nodes.label: Weekly Fail-Closed Article Fan-Out
nodes.rationale: A Monday 06:00 UTC run ranks all passing candidates, generates at most the configured top three, opens one draft pull request per article, and emits no pull request when any safety or budget gate fails.

nodes.label: Grounded Article Image Policy
nodes.rationale: Articles may use generated body images and allowlisted source images only with visible attribution, server-side URL validation, and an enforced RAI and copyright review checklist.

nodes.label: Tavily Key Rotation Run Gate
nodes.rationale: The previously pasted Tavily key is treated as compromised; rotation and storage only as TAVILY_API_KEY are blocking prerequisites for the first real weekly run.

nodes.label: English Processing and Spanish Publishing
nodes.rationale: All AI reasoning and working metadata use English in a single pass, while every reader-facing field remains Spanish from Spain and image prompts remain English.

nodes.label: Eager and Anticipatory Agent Routing
nodes.rationale: Useful agents start eagerly, downstream testing is anticipated, substantial work always starts a background Scribe, and quick facts stay with the coordinator.
